content: Investigation Summary: The incident occurred on March 17, 2024, at Sector 45 pedestrian crossing. The vehicle involved is registered to Priya Sharma, but evidence indicates her husband, Rohan Sharma, was the driver. Digital forensics confirm Rohan’s presence at the scene via Bluetooth and GPS. His alibi via geotagged photos was fabricated. The mechanical inspection report was falsified by the garage owner, who admitted to altering the damage age upon request. The paint fragment does not match the currently registered vehicle, suggesting the vehicle had been modified with an older headlight and paint. Witness Anjali Kapoor’s testimony of a female driver is inconsistent with CCTV and appears to be a misidentification due to poor eyesight. Vikas Mehta’s alibi is partially corroborated by bus ticket and station CCTV, but his phone was paired with Rohan’s at the time of impact. Financial records confirm Arjun Mehta was about to expose a fraud involving Vikas and Priya’s law firm. Motive: To silence Arjun Mehta. Suspects: Rohan Sharma (driver), Vikas Mehta (conspirator). Priya Sharma’s alibi via Zoom is compromised — her screen recording shows a 4-minute gap (18:58–19:02) where she was not visible. Her insurance claim filed two days after the incident suggests awareness of damage. Case is recommended for charge sheet under Sections 304A, 201, 120B IPC and MV Act violations. Recommend arrest of Rohan Sharma and Vikas Mehta. Priya Sharma to be questioned further.
